Hey guys, welcome back. So now we're going to dive into the theme plates for the launch sequence. And the first one, theme plate number eight is what I call the calm before or the never too early ultimatum. And this is going to be that pulse point number two, which we spoke about earlier. You know, there are three of them. The first one was that third call out email where we just really kind of ask them, you know, like if this isn't something you want, you know, feel free to check out. And this is one where we're getting that buy in again. It's really that equivalent of, you know, a coach who right before getting into the offer says, you know, is this something you'd be interested in hearing about? So it's really about that gut check, getting that buy in and doing so at that crucial time right before your offer comes, you know, that's where you want to reaffirm that commitment, get that buy in, you know, and really build that trust and intimacy where you're frankly going to need it most. So that's what we're doing here. We're giving them that chance to be removed and opt out once again, because if they don't remove themselves, it implies that they want to be in. And that also triggers a bit of that consistency factor. If they've chosen, if they've decided they want to be here, knowing full well, they're going to be exposed to an invitation and an opportunity the next day, it does reinforce that consistency. So that's how we're working with here. Here is the container we're going to be using to deliver this content. We're going to start off by building intimacy and connection. And you could do this any way you feel appropriate. I find just bringing people into your reality is intimate, you know, opening that window and saying, I am here. This is exactly what I'm doing and feeling, you know, that's the simplest way. To build intimacy, just be totally transparent about where you're at. And now instead, your program is going live the next morning. So this is an email that goes out the night before you open cart. And just very quickly touch on the pain they'll escape from. You know, you don't have to pour it on to thick here. You've already done that a lot in your pre-launch. This really, you don't want to break away too far from that intimacy, but you do want to remind them of the pain they're going to be escaping from as well as foreshadow and reinforce the big outcome. Then you could put in that call to action and ask them to reconfirm their interests. Say, is this something you're interested in hearing about? Is this still cool? Get that buy-in. Give yourself permission to continue emailing them so that you can feel invited instead of just like, uh, I don't really want to sell, send another sales email. I don't know if they're interested. This is where you give yourself that confidence to keep being in their inbox where you continue to feel invited to share your offer. And then a second call to action can just remind them of that value you've already given in the pre-launch. And then you give them that escape link. You know, if this isn't what you want, you know, right after you've reminded them of the value you've given them. So that's what's really kind of like creating that reciprocity principle. You know, you're reminding them of everything you've provided. That should trigger a wanting this for them to reciprocate not to escape. So you're, um, I wouldn't say gaming the system. That sounds way too manipulative, but you're just being honest. I've given you this so far. Um, if it's valuable, if it's something you really, really want, you're in. But if not, here is your chance to opt out before, um, before tomorrow, before the offer goes live. So this is how it's going to play out.
